title: Multi-thread depth on large deals doubled since Q1
anchor: Active opportunities above $250K ACV average 4.2 engaged contacts in Q2, up from 2.1 contacts in Q1.
connect: Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% versus 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
body: Active opportunities above $250K ACV average 4.2 engaged contacts in Q2, up from 2.1 contacts in Q1. Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% versus 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
trace: Multi-thread depth improvement on $250K+ deals correlates with win rate advantage, suggesting buying committee engagement strategy is working.

title: Sterling enterprise committee expanded during evaluation
anchor: Executive demo on April 18th grew the buying committee from 4 to 7 contacts, adding CFO and two business-unit leads.
connect: Historical close rate for committees that grow during evaluation runs 26% above flat committees.
body: Executive demo on April 18th grew the buying committee from 4 to 7 contacts, adding CFO and two business-unit leads. Historical close rate for committees that grow during evaluation runs 26% above flat committees.
trace: Sterling deal shows positive momentum with committee expansion post-demo, pattern matches to higher-converting deal profile.

title: Champion re-engagement triggers stage advancement within three weeks
anchor: 14 champion re-engagement events in April resulted in 9 deals advancing a stage within 21 days.
connect: Previous re-engagement event was March 6th with similar advancement patterns.
body: 14 champion re-engagement events in April resulted in 9 deals advancing a stage within 21 days. Previous re-engagement event was March 6th with similar advancement patterns.
trace: Champion re-engagement strategy showing consistent results across multiple events, 64% advancement rate within three weeks.

title: Q3 pipeline coverage sits at 3.4x quota

title: Single-threaded deal concentration drops 12 points

connect: This shift coincides with the multi-thread depth improvement on large deals.
body: Single-threaded coverage share sits at 22% in Q2 versus 34% in Q1. This shift coincides with the multi-thread depth improvement on large deals.
trace: Reduction in single-threaded deals aligns with increased multi-threading on large opportunities, reducing concentration risk.

title: Proposal-stage aging clears with re-issued documents

connect: Proposal-to-closed median for enterprise deals compressed to 38 days in Q2 from 49 days in Q1.

trace: Proposal reissuance strategy showing immediate impact on aged deals, contributing to overall cycle compression.

title: Mid-market win rate climbs 8 points with ICP alignment

body: 51% of active opportunities carry named Champion fields in CRM during Q2. Win rate with Champion documentation runs at 38% versus 21% without Champion field.

title: Revised outbound sequence conversion jumps to 14%
